dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal uses z-score thresholds (1 and 0) which map to Bollinger Bands %B indicator. Entry at z-score ±1 corresponds to bb_pct_b > 1.0 (long, above upper band) and bb_pct_b < 0.0 (short, below lower band). Exit at z-score 0 (mean) corresponds to bb_pct_b ≈ 0.5 (middle band). Lookback period, half-life, and backtest parameters marked AUTHOR_SILENT are not included in rules but would typically be 20 periods for Bollinger Bands. This is a mean-reversion strategy trading both sides bidirectionally.

Entry at z-score ±1 corresponds to bb_pct_b > 1.0 (long, above upper band) and bb_pct_b < 0.0 (short, below lower band). Exit at z-score 0 (mean) corresponds to bb_pct_b ≈ 0.5 (middle band). Lookback period, half-life, and backtest parameters marked AUTHOR_SILENT are not included in rules but would typically be 20 periods for Bollinger Bands. This is a mean-reversion strategy trading both sides bidirectionally.

source_rule_text: A mean-reversion strategy using Bollinger Bands that enters when z-score exceeds 1 and exits when z-score returns to 0, trading both long and short sides of a spread.
